title: alcea lavateriflora var. kirrindensis
intro: Alcea lavateriflora var. kirrindensis, also known as the Himalayan hollyhock, is a perennial plant native to the Himalayan region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Alcea lavateriflora var. kirrindensis are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments.
